findings.title: Disk usage command treats invalid or spaced paths as successful output
findings.impact: When data_dir is missing, unreadable, or contains spaces, du can fail or interpret the path as multiple operands, but the function ignores the non-zero return code and returns only stripped stdout. Callers can receive an empty string or a report for the wrong path and treat it as a valid worker disk-usage result.
findings.fix: Invoke du with an argument list such as subprocess.run(["du", "-sh", data_dir], check=True, capture_output=True, text=True) and propagate or convert CalledProcessError into the repository's expected CLI error path instead of returning stdout unconditionally.
